payload.claim_text: 14th Street is a designated truck route. Allowing trucks helps to limit through truck traffic on side streets.
Left turn bans keep buses from getting stuck behind turning vehicles and increase pedestrian safety; left turns are associated with high rates of pedestrian injuries
Pickups and drop-offs are allowed throughout the corridor
payload.description: Passenger vehicles may drop-off and pickup all along the corridor

description: Transit & Truck Priority corridor on 14th Street restricted to buses, trucks, and emergency vehicles 6 AM–10 PM. Part of Vision Zero Priority Corridor.
14th Street between 9th Avenue and 3rd Avenue designated as a Transit & Truck Priority corridor for an 18-month pilot project.
Major connector between the Lower East Side, Union Square and the Meatpacking District. Busway operates between 3rd Avenue and 9th Avenue with bus priority from 9th Avenue to 1st Avenue.

Vision Zero Priority Corridor. From 6 AM–10 PM, only buses, trucks, and emergency vehicles are allowed. Other vehicles for local trips must make next available right turn. All left turns restricted except for MTA buses at certain intersections.
14th Street corridor in Manhattan with transit and truck priority restrictions including curb access rules and turn restrictions

Vision Zero Priority Corridor. From 6 AM–10 PM, only buses, trucks, and emergency vehicles allowed. Other vehicles allowed for local trips and garage access but must make next available right turn. All left turns restricted except for MTA buses at certain intersections.
BRT Phase II candidate corridor.
Difficult Trip corridor identified in BRT Phase II report; existing M14 bus route carries 39,000 trips a day
Ranked highest among all respondents in Manhattan survey; Manhattan residents preferred the Upper West Side/Upper East Side route to the 14th Street route
limits: 3rd Avenue to 8th Avenue (westbound) and 9th Avenue to 3rd Avenue (eastbound)
3rd Avenue to 8th Avenue westbound, 9th Avenue to 3rd Avenue eastbound

records.raw_text: As part of the Bronx Bus Network Redesign planning process, MTA and NYCDOT conducted an analysis of major Bronx corridors to identify streets where bus lanes and other bus priority treatments would provide the biggest benefit to Bronx bus riders.

payload.description: M14A/D Select Bus Service began operation
14th Street Busway was made permanent after successful pilot, with a plan to extend bus lanes east

The busway pilot increased bus speeds by as much as 24%

payload.description: The busway pilot increased ridership by as much as 30%

description: Permanent busway on Manhattan's 14th Street between 3rd Avenue and 9th Avenue, made permanent in June 2020 after a successful pilot
Better Buses Restart extended and made permanent the 14th Street Busway. Travel speeds increased up to 47% after installation in 2019, making trips 10 minutes faster.
A bus-only street in Lower Manhattan, from 6 a.m. to 10 p.m., only trucks and buses are allowed to travel through the corridor. Started as a pilot in 2019, later made permanent.

payload.description: Permanent busway on Manhattan's 14th Street between 3rd Avenue and 9th Avenue, made permanent in June 2020 after a successful pilot

records.display_name: 14th Street Transit & Truck Priority Pilot Project

description: Implemented by NYCDOT in October 2019, aiming to improve operations of the M14 A and M14 D Select Bus Service (M14 A/D SBS), increase safety on this Vision Zero Priority Corridor, and maintain the street as an important truck route. From 6 AM–10 PM, only buses, trucks, and emergency vehicles allowed on 14th Street from 3rd Avenue to 8th Avenue westbound and 9th Avenue to 3rd Avenue eastbound.
18-month pilot project making 14th Street between 9th Avenue and 3rd Avenue a Transit & Truck Priority corridor starting October 3, 2019.
Pilot project implemented by NYCDOT in October 2019 aiming to improve operations of M14A/D Select Bus Service, increase safety on Vision Zero Priority Corridor, and maintain street as truck route
NYC DOT piloted a Transit and Truck Priority design on 14th Street, formerly known as the 14th Street Transit and Truck Priority Pilot Project, which later became the permanent 14th Street Busway

NYCDOT will pilot a Transit & Truck Priority design on 14th Street between 3rd Avenue and 9th Avenue, and will also install standard bus lanes between 1st Avenue and 3rd Avenue, coinciding with SBS launch for M14A and M14D buses in Summer 2019
14th Street Transit & Truck Priority pilot project with bus and truck priority access restrictions, curb access rules, and turn restrictions

The 14th Street Transit & Truck Priority (TTP) Pilot Project was implemented by NYCDOT in October 2019, aiming to improve operations of the M14 A and M14 D Select Bus Service (M14 A/D SBS). Also aims to increase safety on this Vision Zero Priority Corridor while maintaining the street as an important truck route.

payload.description: NYC DOT informational webpage about the 14th Street Busway, formerly the 14th Street Transit and Truck Priority Pilot Project, covering rules, background, FAQ, and community outreach
payload.title: 14th Street Busway

payload.description: All vehicles are restricted from making left turns off of 14th Street (except MTA buses at signed locations)
Restrictions are enforced through automated cameras along 14th Street. NYPD traffic agents may also issue summonses. Automated cameras may only be used for bus lane enforcement.
Between 10 PM to 6 AM, all vehicles may make thru trips along 14th Street
Every day between 6 AM and 10 PM, only buses, trucks and emergency vehicles may travel along Manhattan's 14th Street between 3rd Avenue and 9th Avenue
All other vehicles may make local trips to access businesses, residences and garages along 14th Street, but drivers must make the next available right turn

Commercial vehicles may load and unload in short-term metered loading zones. Short-term loading zones are provided along the corridor every day from 6 am to 10 pm; meters are in effect Monday to Saturday.
The project combines blocks of exclusive access and standard bus lanes to provide bus priority from 9th Avenue to 1st Avenue
A truck is any vehicle designed to transport property that has more than two axles or six or more tires. 14th Street is a designated truck route.
